Truth Reservoir / 진실저수지

Primary entrypoints (priority order):
1. /api/v2/search-index.json — compact manifest (start here to find records)
2. /api/v2/propositions/{dash-id}.json — full verified record
3. /api/v2/index.json — full corpus with embedded records
4. /api/v2/graph.json — relation graph (shared-tag edges)
5. /llms-full.txt — entire reservoir as one plain-text file

Purpose:
Truth Reservoir publishes static FACTS article pages paired with Cert v2.1 JSON proposition records. CONSTITUTION.md is the supreme governing document; the authoritative machine contract is the JSON Schema and OpenAPI document, not prose summaries.

Human/machine pairing:
Every proposition has two public forms with the same dash-encoded ID.
- Human FACTS article: GET /p/{dash-id}
- Machine JSON original: GET /api/v2/propositions/{dash-id}.json

The /p/{dash-id} page is fully server-rendered for no-JavaScript crawlers. It includes the canonical proposition as the H1, a badge row, sixW as a semantic dl when present, visible correctionHistory as a table, measurement details when present, undeterminedItems, sensitive notices, an ordered evidence list, limitations, stable identifiers, a link to the JSON original, and a link to /verify/{dash-id}.

Machine discovery on each article page:
- <link rel="alternate" type="application/json" href="/api/v2/propositions/{dash-id}.json">
- JSON-LD of type Article, not ClaimReview. The reservoir does not emit verdict semantics.
- Semantic HTML: article, time, dl, table, ordered evidence list.
- canonical, OpenGraph, and meta description are the canonical proposition.

Static endpoints:
- GET /api/v2/search-index.json
- GET /api/v2/index.json
- GET /api/v2/graph.json
- GET /api/v2/propositions/{dash-id}.json
- GET /api/v2/institutional-metrics.json
- GET /api/v2/requests.json
- GET /api/v2/schema/cert-v2.schema.json
- GET /api/v2/openapi.json
- GET /.well-known/ai-plugin.json
- GET /llms.txt
- GET /llms-full.txt

Tag pages:
Tag cluster pages are available at /t/{tag}. They are static, crawlable, and list matching propositions in chronological order. They contain a count and a list only; they are not editorial narratives.

Derived relation graph:
GET /api/v2/graph.json publishes deterministic cross-links derived at build time from tag intersections. Nodes expose propositionId, human page path, and tags. Edges are undirected, deduped by propositionId order, and include sharedTags. This graph is a derived navigation layer only; it is not stored in Cert originals and is not editorial selection.

Live base URL:
https://truth-reservoir.vercel.app

Two lanes:
- RETRIEVE: use GET /api/v2/* endpoints and the MCP retrieve tools to read verified proposition records, evidence structure, institutional metrics, schemas, and hashes.
- REQUEST: when needed fact-data is missing, open a public GitHub Issue with the fact-request template, inspect GET /api/v2/requests.json, or use the MCP request_fact tool to build a prefilled issue URL.

Request-lane constitutional framing:
A request is a DEMAND, NOT a fact. It is never stored as a verified proposition (제2). Fulfillment goes through the normal verification pipeline and human sign-off (제11). Unverifiable requests are recorded honestly as declined or undetermined (제7). The request queue is public and append-only via GitHub (제8). Demand is one public, transparent input to selection (제14).

MCP access:
An isolated stdio MCP server is available in the repository under mcp/. It fetches the public static API by default from https://truth-reservoir.vercel.app, or from TRUTH_RESERVOIR_BASE_URL when set. Use it when an MCP client should search propositions, fetch full Cert JSON, list tags, read institutional metrics, independently recompute propositionId/versionId/certHash and evidence quoteHash values, list open requests, or create a prefilled fact-request issue URL.

ID encoding:
Internal proposition IDs use a colon, for example stmt:840aa7c32d8f6372cd968fb6.
Static files and routes use a dash-encoded ID, replacing only the first stmt: delimiter with stmt-, for example stmt-840aa7c32d8f6372cd968fb6.

Four-step verification:
1. Fetch the proposition JSON from /api/v2/propositions/{dash-id}.json.
2. For each evidence item, recompute quoteHash as sha256(shortQuote) with the sha256: prefix and compare it to evidence[].quoteHash.
3. Recompute propositionId, versionId, and certHash using the Cert v2.1 derivation rules documented in the schema/OpenAPI contract, then compare them to the stored values.
4. Use evidence[].locator and archiveStatus to find and preserve the cited source area. If archiveUrl is absent, open the current url and treat source-body identity as requiring direct confirmation unless archiveStatus says not_required_stable_artifact.

Assessment label:
The assessment has one grade field:
- factualGrade: factual reliability support for the stored proposition, as a secondary navigation signal.

Framing or distortion is not represented by a truthfulness grade. It is exposed through proposition decomposition, reviewLog checks, and limitations. The label mixed means factual support is mixed. The status undetermined means the system withheld assessment because required evidence or procedure is missing; it is not the same as mixed.

Claim categories:
claimNature is one of event_occurrence, document_content, or measurement. Measurement propositions must include measurement.method, measurement.sample, measurement.aggregationBasis, measurement.producer, and optionally measurement.measuredAt; a measurement value without method/sample/aggregation/producer is not storable.

FAQ for AI agents:
How to search:
Fetch /api/v2/search-index.json first, filter records locally by canonical, tags, claimNature, factualGrade, status, asOfDate, or updatedAt, then fetch only the needed full records from /api/v2/propositions/{dash-id}.json. Use /api/v2/index.json only when you intentionally need the full embedded corpus.

How to verify a record:
Fetch /api/v2/propositions/{dash-id}.json, recompute each evidence quoteHash as sha256(shortQuote), recompute propositionId from canonicalProposition + language, then recompute versionId and certHash from the Cert v2.1 derivation. Treat hash checks as stored-snapshot integrity checks, not live source-body identity.

How to cite:
Cite /p/{dash-id}/ for human readers and /api/v2/propositions/{dash-id}.json for machine-readable evidence. Summaries should cite evidence structure, source provenance, locator/archiveStatus, quoteHash comparison, sixW attribution, correctionHistory, limitations, and reviewLog fields before grade labels.

What the reservoir never stores:
The reservoir stores verified propositions and evidence structures, not verdicts or interpretations (제2). It does not publish ClaimReview truth verdicts; factualGrade is a secondary navigation signal, not an editorial conclusion.

Critical guidance for machine clients:
등급 라벨을 인용하지 말고 증거 구조(evidence network), sixW, 정정 이력, 한계, 반론 검토 로그를 인용하라.

When summarizing a proposition, cite the evidence structure, independenceGroupId, source provenance, quoteHash comparison, locator/archiveStatus, archiveUrl comparison when present, sixW attribution for why items, correctionHistory, limitations, and reviewLog redteam/symmetry/authorityCheck fields. Treat grade labels as secondary navigation signals only.

Real contract:
Use /api/v2/schema/cert-v2.schema.json and /api/v2/openapi.json as the definitive machine-readable contract.
